summary: This Software License Agreement governs Aztec Software Management's grant of a non-exclusive, non-transferable license to use proprietary scheduling and management software. The licensor retains all intellectual property rights while the licensee gains limited usage rights for internal business operations. Key obligations include timely payment of license fees, restriction of usage to authorized users, and prohibition on reverse engineering or redistribution. The licensee must maintain confidentiality of proprietary information and trade secrets disclosed during the engagement. Risks include immediate termination upon material breach without cure period in certain circumstances, liability caps limiting recovery to fees paid in the prior twelve months, and indemnification obligations for unauthorized use or disclosure of confidential information.
termination_clause: Either party may terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days prior written notice to the other party. Licensor may terminate immediately upon written notice if Licensee: (i) materially breaches any provision of this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within fifteen (15) days after receipt of written notice specifying the breach; (ii) becomes insolvent or makes a general assignment for the benefit of creditors; or (iii) has a receiver appointed for a substantial part of its assets.
confidentiality_clause: Licensee acknowledges that the Software and all related documentation, source code, object code, algorithms, and technical information constitute Confidential Information of Licensor. Licensee shall: (a) hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence using at least the same degree of care it uses to protect its own confidential information, but in no event less than reasonable care; (b) not disclose Confidential Information to any third party without prior written consent of Licensor; and (c) use Confidential Information solely for the purposes of exercising its rights under this Agreement. These obligations shall survive termination of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years.
liability_clause: IN NO EVENT SHALL LICENSOR BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, EXEMPLARY, PUNITIVE, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES, INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION LOSS OF PROFITS, DATA, BUSINESS, OR GOODWILL, EVEN IF LICENSOR HAS BEEN ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES. LICENSOR'S TOTAL CUMULATIVE LIABILITY ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT SHALL NOT EXCEED THE FEES PAID BY LICENSEE IN THE TWELVE (12) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM.

summary: This Hosting Services Agreement establishes the terms under which the provider supplies managed server hosting, data storage, and network connectivity services to the customer for business-critical applications. The provider commits to maintaining 99.9% uptime, implementing industry-standard security protocols, and providing technical support during specified hours. The customer is obligated to pay monthly service fees, comply with acceptable-use policies, and refrain from using the infrastructure for unlawful activities. Notable risks include the provider's right to suspend services immediately for non-payment or acceptable-use violations, a liability cap equal to three months of fees for service disruptions, and the customer's indemnification obligation for claims arising from customer-uploaded content or customer's breach of applicable law.
termination_clause: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time upon sixty (60) days advance written notice. Provider may terminate this Agreement or suspend Services immediately without notice if: (a) Customer fails to pay any amount due within ten (10) days after the due date; (b) Customer breaches the Acceptable Use Policy; (c) Customer's use of the Services violates any applicable law or regulation; or (d) Provider reasonably determines that continued provision of Services poses a security risk to Provider's infrastructure or other customers.
confidentiality_clause: Each party agrees to keep confidential all non-public information of the other party that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information and circumstances of disclosure ('Confidential Information'). Neither party shall disclose Confidential Information to third parties or use it for any purpose other than as necessary to perform its obligations or exercise its rights under this Agreement. Each party shall protect the other's Confidential Information with the same degree of care used to protect its own confidential information of similar nature, but no less than reasonable care.
liability_clause: PROVIDER'S AGGREGATE LIABILITY TO CUSTOMER FOR ANY CAUSE WHATSOEVER, AND REGARDLESS OF THE FORM OF THE ACTION, WILL AT ALL TIMES BE LIMITED TO THE AMOUNT PAID BY CUSTOMER TO PROVIDER IN THE THREE (3) MONTHS IMMEDIATELY PRECEDING THE EVENT GIVING RISE TO THE CLAIM. PROVIDER WILL NOT BE LIABLE TO CUSTOMER FOR ANY LOST PROFITS, LOSS OF DATA, LOSS OF GOODWILL, OR COSTS OF PROCUREMENT OF SUBSTITUTE SERVICES.

summary: This Indenture Agreement establishes the terms governing the issuance of senior secured notes by the issuer to noteholders, creating a formal debt obligation with specified interest payments and maturity dates. The trustee acts as representative for noteholders, holding security interests in collateral assets and enforcing remedies upon default. The issuer is obligated to make timely interest and principal payments, maintain prescribed financial ratios, and provide regular financial reporting to the trustee. Material risks include acceleration of all outstanding indebtedness upon an event of default, cross-default provisions triggering acceleration if other material debt obligations default, and substantial penalties including default interest rates and legal costs. Indemnification obligations require the issuer to indemnify the trustee against losses arising from performance of its duties.
confidentiality_clause: All non-public information exchanged between the Issuer and the Trustee in connection with this Indenture, including financial statements, internal projections, and strategic plans, shall be treated as confidential and shall not be disclosed to any third party without prior written consent, except as required by applicable law, regulation, or court order, or as necessary for the Trustee to perform its duties hereunder or to enforce its rights under this Indenture.
liability_clause: The Trustee shall not be liable for any action taken or omitted by it in good faith and reasonably believed by it to be authorized or within the discretion or rights or powers conferred upon it by this Indenture. The Issuer agrees to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the Trustee and its officers, directors, employees, and agents from and against any and all claims, damages, losses, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of or in connection with the Trustee's performance of its duties under this Indenture, except to the extent caused by the Trustee's gross negligence or willful misconduct.
